title: Week 25
summary:
Capesize
An upbeat week generally for the big ships but there remained a fragility about the market that even with rates rising, some became increasingly nervous about how long it would last. The West Australia/China rate certainly reached $5.75 but it was never established whether $6.00 was agreed. Despite rumours of Rio Tinto booking many vessels Thursday, BHP Billiton today paid $5.45 for a ship to move a 29-30 June 170,000 tonne 10% cargo from Port Hedland to Qingdao and took an earlier newcastlemax today at $5.60.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: An upbeat week generally for the big ships but there remained a fragility about the market that even with rates rising, some became increasingly nervous about how long it would last. The West Australia/China rate certainly reached $5.75 but it was never established whether $6.00 was agreed.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: South American activity has largely shaped activity generally. As charterers piled in to take tonnage absorbing ballasters from the East and ships from the North Atlantic, rates rose quickly.

children.title: Supramax / Handysize
children.summary: A more encouraging week for supramaxes in most areas. In the Atlantic the US Gulf markets continued to push and although the week ended on a quiet note, rates being discussed were still thought to be relatively firm.
